title: Module 2 knowledge check
markdown: Check your understanding of data, training vs. inference, and learning types.

questions.prompt: Using a trained model to classify a brand-new email as spam or not is primarily:
choices: Unsupervised clustering of the entire internet

questions.prompt: A feature in machine learning is best described as:
choices: An input signal or measurable property the model uses
Proof that the model understands ethics
A human teacher sitting next to the computer

questions.prompt: Predicting house prices from past sales with known prices is an example of:
Grouping customers into segments without pre-assigned labels is closest to:
Overfitting means a model performs perfectly on new, unseen data by definition.

choices: A trained model captures patterns learned from data; it is not a verified fact database.
Training and inference are the same step.
Features are optional for all ML systems.
